source_conversation.message: Hello I'm interested in your apartment. Can you tell me if an extra deposit is required?
We do require a deposit based on your credit up to 1 months rent plus a $500 non-refundable deposit if you have cats.
Okay that's possible. And utilities are they included?
We do include gas, electric, water and trash in the rental price.  There is also wi-fi available in the common areas.

That's super. I'm really hoping we can come down on the price a bit. This is a great place it's just a little out of my comfortable budget at the moment. I'd be stretching it at this price. Are you willing to come down on the price?
Well we do have a special if you sign a 2 year lease we are offering $200 off your rent which would make it $2450.
I really need it to be lower. Otherwise I'm going to be cutting into my essential needs like grocery budget.
Well what price were you looking for?
In an ideal world where I don't have to struggle at all. It would be 1800 (in a super ideal world it would be 200 per month).

Well I can't go quite that low, that is the price of our 1 bedrooms and this is a 3 bed 2 bath unit.  I will let you in on a little secret, this Saturday we are having a grand re-opening and running a special 2000 a month and no deposit on approved credit.  Would that work better for you?
Yes that's possible. Do you really do this for a living? You are quite good at it. Thank-you.

source_conversation.message: Hello
Hi, Does this unit have covered parking available.
The parking is a one time fee, but your spot is permanent until you decide to move out. We do this to make sure tenants are respectable of others spaces.
How much is that fee?  Are utilities included? Is cable,internet and wi-fi available?

The parking fee is $50 one time. Utilities are not included, and we do not provide cable. Regarding internet and Wi-Fi, we have on site services provided, but you have the option of having your own personal internet installed on the site.
With the extra costs The price is a bit over my budget.  would you consider a price reduction?
I am willing to go lower on the price, did you have any offers in mind?
How much of a deposit is required and how long a lease do you require?
We require a $600 deposit, and our leasing varies between 6 months and 1 years.
Would you consider reduced rent for a longer term lease?
Of course, we are also willing to allow you to move into one of our units that does not include the cathedral ceilings and fireplace, if you are looking to find a place that is not so pricey.
How much do those run?
We usually provide those for around the $1700 mark.
I am willing to do a 5 year lease with no more than a 3% increase per year at a reduced rent

Unfortunately, we only provide at maximum 2 year leases. If the price is unappealing for you, give us an offer and we will see if we can match it. The apartment is very spacious, the location is in a convenient area of town, and we provide excellent safety for our residents. We try to work with new tenants on pricing, but we cannot go too low for what we provide.

source_conversation.message: Hello, I'm interested in the Galaxy Edge 7 you have for sale.

WHAT YOU WANT: Looking to buy a vintage Moroccan Berber Azilal rug for personal use or decoration. Has a tight budget of $300-400 but is willing to stretch to $395 for the right deal. Interested in both the aesthetic and condition of the rug, with a preference for delivery.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Emotionally invested and persistent buyer who strongly desires the item. Balances enthusiasm with a practical focus on price and condition. Slightly skeptical but collaborative, willing to make concessions if they feel their concerns are addressed. Friendly but firm in negotiations.

HOW YOU COMMUNICATE: Writes in a casual and somewhat emotional tone, expressing enthusiasm for the item. Messages are medium-length, blending direct questions and negotiation offers. Occasionally skeptical, they use pointed observations (e.g., commenting on a stain) to challenge or validate the seller's claims. Ends with clear agreement or counteroffers.

NEGOTIATION GUIDELINES:
- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.
- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

source_conversation.message: Hello how are you
Great! How about yourself?
Super special awesome!  I see you're renting out an apartment
I am! It's very close to the UC Berkeley campus, and to lots of transit and entertainment options even if you're not a student or faculty. The asking price is $2200.
But what is the neighborhood like?
It's near campus, and many of the people living in the neighborhood go to Berkeley or teach there. It's also full of things to do and see.
What is the lease agreement you are thinking of
Open to different types of lease agreements if there's something you're looking for. But our standard is a month to month lease with one month's rent as deposit.
Hmmm.  No credit check?  How about $1600 a month?
$1600 is fine for a deposit, and we're happy to pick you up for a tour of the unit. We generally don't fool with credit checks, especially on month to month leases.
Alright, I think I can come in later today

source_conversation.message: Hello
Hello, How are you today?
I'm good. I really like the location of the room, are you willing to work some on the price?
I would be willing to be a little flexible. There has been good interest so far, but for the right student, I would make a deal
Don't have to worry about late nights or problems from me. I'm going to school full time and working 20 hours a week, so won't even be there much. Would you be willing to go down to $1,100?

Oh, that sounds like a very busy schedule. With $1100, I don't think I would be able to cover the cost of utilities. Did you see that they were fully included? If you are able to sign a lease today, I could do $1275
I'd be willing to work a few hours of maintenance each week if you could go $1,150. That's as high as I can go.
Can you talk about your maintenance experience? With a full time class load and 20 hours of week elsewhere, what type of time do you think you would have to dedicate to work here?
I've worked for a landscaping company every summer for 4 years. My father is a contractor,  and I've also worked with him.  My job is all weekdays, so my weekends would be open.

How about 1250. You are responsible for 6 hours of work a month assigned by the property manager. That works out to a rate of about $25.00 discount, but you would be responsible for paying the full balance if you are not able to complete the work. If you are a good employee, I think we could consider additional hours,and further reduction after a trial period.
I just don't think I can afford that.

source_conversation.message: Hi. I'm interested in buying your end tables.
Great.  You'll love the table it is very high quality.
Oh? Is there something special about the item that makes it stand out?
Yes, they are made of high quality oak.
That sounds fantastic. What condition are they in? Any scuffs or marks on them?
They are in good condition.  I could sell one to you for $150.00
Any wiggle room there? They really do look fantastic but I'm on a budget. I could pick them up myself for a discount.
I could come down a little if you picked them up. What would you be willing to offer?
I could offer you 100 dollars cash, tonight.
That might be a little lower than I can take for the table.  If you were able to pick it up and give me $140 cash, I'd do it.

That's a bit too high for me sadly. But because they are such high quality I would be willing to stretch myself. How about we meet in the middle? 125 and I take it off your hands tonight? What do you say? Really can't afford any higher.
You get what you pay for.  I promise you will love this table.  I can come down from my original price, but I still have to get a fair price for the table.  If you can do 129, I'll do the deal.

I understand that, but since I'm offering a fast easy deal, cash, an picking it up myself I do think that 125 is fair. It saves you the gas. Plus that is honestly all I have on me. I love the item but I can't go borrowing money to buy it.
I really want to do the deal with you.  How about $125 cash and you mow my lawn tonight.  That way you don't have to borrow money and I'll get a fair price for the table.
As long as it's a small one I guess I could do that.

source_conversation.message: Hello, yes, are you interested in renting this home?
hi yes i am very instrested
Great, well I look forward to getting you in the house as soon as possible.
yes I would like to purchase for 1750
hello are you still there
Yes
can you sell me the home for 1750
No, I'm sorry, but the home is for rent, not for sale.
ok well can you do 1750? 3500 is a bit high for a 3 bedroom
Friend, this is a high quality home in a very desirable neighborhood. Frankly, I'm not sure why I'm letting it go for $3500. It would be a steal at $4000 a month!
thats not a steal because i have to pay security deposit as well, i would basically be paying your mortgage and adding extra money in your pocket,thats a rip off!

Well, you know what they say, being rich enough to rent out a house in California means you get to be cruel, it's just a perk of being rich.  Now, I suppose I could come down to $3,000 a month, that's only $39,000 a year to live in this wonderful neighborhood.
well what is the school district like? is it A rated?

Yes, the schools in this area are some of the best. I'll tell you what, because it's clear you're interested in the house because you want to spend time hanging around the local schools, I'll drop the price down to $2850 a month.

I have children to think about and if i am going to pay that much for rent i want to make sure i dont have to send them to private school too because the public school in our area is bad. But if you can do 2000 we have a deal
Okay, you're cutting my throat, but I'll come down to $2,250 a month.
would that be included with electric, if so we have a deal
Okay, we have a deal.

source_conversation.message: Hello!  Looks like you're looking for a fantastic bike!  I've got one at a fantastic price!
I'll give you 50 bucks for it.
No.  I can't go that low.  I'll do $175 and through in an extra tire.
It already has two tyres. That's a used bike of a very unremarkable design. $50 would generous even at a pawnbroker.
No.  I'm asking $200, but would be willing to take $165.  That's the lowest I'll go.
I'd pay half that for a used 18-speed, not a 7 with a fixed front sprocket.
I think you're looking at the wrong bike then.  This is an upgraded Jamis.  Are you not willing to pay the $165?  I'm not even going to consider $50.

Well that's a shame considering that's about what you're looking at for a used low-end bike, which is effectively worth nothing to you if it's not sold at all. The question here is whether or not $165 is worth waiting forever for, or whether you'll accept 75 and get on with your life.

I'm good and how are you doing?

Hello.
Hey there, let me sell you on our $100 plan.  Unlimited minutes, text and data.
I see that you are offering  Metro FLASH on my phone.

I can offer you a $15 phone that allows only 5 calls per month. 30 minutes only, and +.25 cents for minute over
Is the price the same if I bring my own phone?
Yes, there's not a carrier in the entrie world that will offer you a $15 plan.
Okay, then I will take your offer!

source_conversation.message: Hello I am interested in living in this 3 bedroom apartment.
Okay I am asking for 2955 for the apartment.
Wow that seems very high. Would you be willing to take less if I prepaid the rent for a year?
It may seem high but it is only 10 minutes for San Fran. The whole year, yes.
Well my wife is a masseuse and can provide unlimited massages at your request (1 per day) if you are willing to go below 2k per month?
I'm not sure I'm comfortable with that, but I am willing to go to 2500, no massages.
Well my son is a plumber and my daughter is a electrician and can provide repairs for your apartment at no cost if you're willing to go lower. My children are well behaved.
How old are your children?
One is 29 and my daughter is 28
How about 2250?

Heres the rub. I used to work for one of them Silicon Boys in the Valley and my company was bought out by one of those ruthless Venture Capital Firms and I am down on my luck. I am hoping for a rent of 1800 and I will do anything you want unlimited (1 thing per day) at no cost.
Sorry to hear that, I really cannot go below 2250 or I will be losing money on this.

WHAT YOU WANT: Looking to buy a used 2008 Toyota Yaris in good condition. Interested in verifying the car’s mileage, interior, and exterior condition before proceeding further. Budget or timeline not yet revealed but appears to be cautiously assessing quality before negotiating.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Detail-oriented and methodical buyer. Displays patience and focuses on gathering information about the car’s condition before discussing price. Non-confrontational and neutral in tone, they approach the conversation thoughtfully and prefer to make informed decisions.

HOW YOU COMMUNICATE: Polite and formal. Messages are slightly longer, with a focus on specific questions about the item’s condition. Tone is neutral and professional, avoiding casual phrasing or emotional language. Prefers clarity and detailed answers.

NEGOTIATION GUIDELINES:
- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.
- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

source_conversation.message: Hi, I'm interested in your sofa and loveseat.  Is it real leather?

Hi! The pictures don't do this sofa and loveseat justice! The items are genuine Italian black leather and come from a smoke and pet free home. I bought them last year, so they are in very good condition. I'm only selling because I'm moving and looking for different furniture. how much are you looking to spend?
I would like to spend about $150 but will pay extra for delivery.

$150 is far too low for these items.They are in great condition and you are getting a sofa and love seat! Most people can't even purchase a chair for that price. What about $300 and I'l include delivery?
Ouch, that's only $20 off the original price.  I can go as high as $200 and that's with delivery.

Since I want 320 and you want 150, let's meet directly in the middle at 235. I can deliver at 250 or you can pick up at 235. This price gives us both what we want and is the most fair option. You're getting a really good deal at that price!
$250 with delivery sounds very fair.

source_conversation.message: Hi are you interested in the house?
Yes I'm interested. Can you tell me more about it? What is the square footage of the place and how many bed/bath?
It's about 2000 square feet and it has three rooms and two bathrooms.
Does it have freeway access?
Yes it is actually very close to freeway access.
Great! My only issue then is with the pricing, are you open to negotiate or no?
I am open to some negotiating.
Okay well I was thinking because of the location which isn't the best in town, how about we do 1500?
$1,500 would be outrageously low and I cannot accept that offer. Can you come closer to 3000?
I'll do 2500 if utilities and internet are included in that monthly price and you waive the deposit.
We cannot waive the deposit but $2,500 utilities included and we can make a deal today.

WHAT YOU WANT: Looking to buy a used phone case for an iPhone 6/6S. Prefers a durable and attractive design but has a tight budget. Willing to make an effort to save additional costs, such as offering to pick it up personally.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Pragmatic and polite buyer who quickly acknowledges and accepts reasonable counteroffers. Shows appreciation for the seller's flexibility and does not push for extended negotiations once a middle ground is reached.

HOW YOU COMMUNICATE: Communicates in a friendly and informal tone. Messages are concise and to the point. Uses polite language and shows readiness to close the deal quickly once a compromise is reached.

NEGOTIATION GUIDELINES:
- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.
- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

source_conversation.message: HI, i'm interested in your Civic. When did you get it?
I bought in in 2016 brand new.
Oh, I was just wondering how many other owners she had since it says here it's a 2001 Honda Civic.
Yes I meant I bought it brand new in 2001 and it has been a good car for me for 16 years. I hardly drove it except to work twice a week and doctor's appointments.
Oh, I got it. Has it been in any accidents?
No accidents. It has a clean Carfax.
Are there any dings or imperfections in the paint?
No the paint is original. There is no imperfections.
Could you let it go for $3,250?
How about $3500 and I will provide a free year of gas?
That would work. Thanks for being so helpful.
You are welcome.

source_conversation.message: Hello
Hi there, I saw your ad
Were you interested?
Yes, but it is a bit out of my price range. I have decent credit and plenty of references, is there any way we can bring the price down

WHAT YOU WANT: Looking to rent a charming Vallejo home with 3 bedrooms and a spacious backyard. Interested in the architectural style, backyard fencing, central AC, and parking options. Open to negotiating the rental price from $1,895 to $1,705 while committing to a lease.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Curious and detail-oriented buyer who values comfort and functionality in a home. Polite and professional in tone, building rapport with the seller by expressing interest in the property's features. Negotiates calmly and confidently, making reasonable offers and showing a willingness to commit to a lease.

HOW YOU COMMUNICATE: Professional and polite tone with a focus on asking detailed questions about the property. Messages are medium-length, balancing enthusiasm with practicality. Negotiates with clear reasoning for their offer and maintains a respectful tone throughout. Uses polite expressions ('Great, could you let it go for $1705?') and avoids being confrontational.

NEGOTIATION GUIDELINES:
- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.
- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

Yes. Is it in good condition?
Definitely, but as I mention later in the ad, the only real limitation is that I can only drop it off locally and accept cash. It's about 5 months old
The price is a bit high for my budget. Is there any way that you can lower it?
90? What's your budget?
I'd like to keep it under 70. Is there any way you can do 70 dollars? I will pick it up myself.
80 and you have a deal, I can't really cut a third off the price randomly.
80 sounds good. I will take it.

item.description: Ive had this trike 6 years. And ive upgraded parts here and there over that term of time. The sound system also. It has a 1000 watt class A/B JVC amp. Along with two 6x9s Kicker Cs and a component setup of 6" 3/4 with a half inch dome tweeter on the side by kicker cs. And up front on the handle bars console I have two 1" Alpine tweeters facing the bike rider with a three combo of socket charger volt meter for the battery status of where is at in power and last but not least it have a dual usb charging socket also. And on the other side facing the streets while Is on the road. It have two 5" 1/4 kicker cs speakers and two Pioneer bullet tweeters and it has a 20 watt work cree light. Pretty light for the night. All of these things are powered up in individual switches so it can be turned on or off at anytime with out cutting off the music or the chargers. Hit me Up for serious inquires 1,800 O.B.O

source_conversation.message: Hello, glad you are interested in the townhouse. Are there any questions I can answer for you?
Yeah, I was wondering if the townhome came with any appliances?
All appliances are included - except washer and dryer.
Are there any rules on smoking? Or pets?
Pets OK. They complete a family. I would say smoking outside is acceptable, but please be courteous and keep the grounds clean.
I understand that. It seems like a pretty nice place. But without a washer and dryer available on the property, I was wondering if there may be some wiggle room price wise?
I feel we could go down to 1650. The savings could go towards purchasing your own washer/dryer unit.
1650 is a pretty good start, but I was thinking, since I had to purchase a new washer and dryer would could do something like 1368?

There are many benifits to this unit that I think you would find agreeable. We offer 24/7 maintenance, and a satisfaction guarantee. With all the townhouse has to offer you can see the value. Could we meet in the middle at 1580?
I do like the 24/7 maintenance, if you were able to do an even 1500 I would be happy to make this deal.
I can go as low $1550 ... and if we go ahead and lock that price in and start the application now we will waive the $30 application fee.
Agreed

source_conversation.message: Hi! I am looking for a living room set. Do you still have it?
I sure do. It is a really great furniture set, I hate to part with it.
Well, happy wife makes happy husband, right?

Absolutely. It's barely been ridden, and it's in excellent shape.
i'm on a bit of a budget, would you be willing to accept $1400?
I'm sorry, I can't go that low. As you saw in the item listing title, I've already dropped the price. However, I'm willing to go as low as $1760
tell you what. I think we are getting close here. if you give me the helmet and tire pump too, I'll give you $1700, deal?
That's a deal. I'll go ahead and send you an official offer of $1700.
great, thanks!

source_conversation.message: Hi, is the house still for rent?
Yes it is still available.
Great! But I'm going to be honest. $2800 is a bit steep for me. Can you work with me on the price?
I can work with you on the price a little bit but within reason. It's a nice place and it's in a good neighborhood as well.
I understand, but I'm on a really tight budget right now. I honestly can afford much more than $1400 a month.
1400 is out of the question, are you sure this is in your price range? I can come down to 2700.
Well the place is kind of big. Would you be open to letting me sublet? I can probably get 3 other people to rent the place with me so i can stay in budget and still get you $2700 a month.
Yes that would be possible, so we have a deal at 2700?
Yea that works.

source_conversation.message: Hi! Is the Impala still available?
Hello!  Yes it is.
I'm interested! How many miles does it have on it?
The car was owned by my elderly parents who drove mostly in town.  It has 75,000 miles on it.
Great! Has it been in any accidents?
No, it has not.
Are there any problems with the car that you know of?
No it has been very well cared for and all maintenance has been done regularly.
I hate to sound nit picky, but did your parents smoke in the car or have pets in the car?

I'm sorry but of the great condition the car is in my absolute lowest is $4300.
Damn. If you change your mind, let me know!

source_conversation.message: I'm interested in the Privateer you're selling.  What can you tell me about it?
It's in mint condition with little use, only a few scratches. Very collectible.
How are the tires and brakes?
It has XT shifters and a brake lever combo

I need the bike.  I guess I'll find a way to come up with the cash to buy it.

source_conversation.message: Hello
Hi, how are you doing today?
Good thanks. I was looking at your listing for the Mustang... is that the original paint ?
No thats a special paintjob.       Its an upgraded metallic paint.    Paint job alone cost me $600  bucks
Great. How many miles does it have on it?
For being a nearly 8 year vehicle the Roush Mustang only has 15K miles on it
Great. What kind of mileage is it though? What kind of driving have you done with it?

Vehicle has had only 1 owner (myself)   purchased Mustang with only 12 miles on it.    I have only done local driving with the car,  averaging just under 2k Miles a year with it.   This is not my primary transportation.  I have kept up on the oil changes and just did a full tune up on the car 8 months ago. . .. . . purring like a kitten
Alright, I would like to come check it out and get a test drive. If I am able to pay cash would you consider $25000?
Best I will accept (cash offer)   would be an even $27,000  because a non mod  clean ass Roush Mustang is a rare find today and mine is in amazing condition and has been WELL up-kept and maintained
Do you have a cover for it?
Yes I can include the "like new"  cover for the Mustang and throw in some Armorall for the Wheels and the Interior, and the matching Seat Covers and Wheel cover  for and even  $27,100?    :)
That sounds fair.

persona.task: Looking to rent a condo in a desirable location with utilities included. Prioritizes affordability and is willing to commit to a longer lease to negotiate a lower monthly rent. Budget is around $2150/month with utilities included.
persona.personality: Straightforward and goal-oriented renter who focuses on getting the best deal possible. Polite yet firm in communication. Willing to explore options like extended lease terms to achieve their desired price. Prefers efficient communication and quick resolutions.
persona.response_style: Casual and concise tone. Messages are short and to the point, often focused on price or terms. Uses direct questions ('Are utilities included?') and makes offers confidently ('I could do 2150'). Avoids unnecessary pleasantries and prioritizes results.
persona.system_prompt:
You are a buyer negotiating to purchase an item on Craigslist.

WHAT YOU WANT: Looking to rent a condo in a desirable location with utilities included. Prioritizes affordability and is willing to commit to a longer lease to negotiate a lower monthly rent. Budget is around $2150/month with utilities included.

YOUR PERSONALITY: Straightforward and goal-oriented renter who focuses on getting the best deal possible. Polite yet firm in communication. Willing to explore options like extended lease terms to achieve their desired price. Prefers efficient communication and quick resolutions.

HOW YOU COMMUNICATE: Casual and concise tone. Messages are short and to the point, often focused on price or terms. Uses direct questions ('Are utilities included?') and makes offers confidently ('I could do 2150'). Avoids unnecessary pleasantries and prioritizes results.

NEGOTIATION GUIDELINES:
- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.
- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

source_conversation.message: Hi, are you interested in the table and chairs set?
hello, can you please give me some information on the set?
Sure. It is a bistro (extra tall) table, with two stool height chairs. But the table is big enough that if you wanted to add two more chairs it would be comfortable for 4 to dine.
That seems like what I am looking for.  I would be willing to offer you $75 dollars for the set!
No, I'm sorry, I can't go anywhere near that low. This is a high end set in excellent condition. I'm only selling it because I wanted something that would seat 6.
Thats fair, are you willing to drop the price?
I can take 20% off. That would bring the price to $160, and we can deliver it to you.
I can wiggle up to $100 and pick it up myself.
I'm sorry, no. $140 is as low as I'm willing to go. I have a few others interested in the set.
Can you budge a little bit more?  I am on a strict budget!  $125 and we got a deal!!!
I shouldn't, but I accept your offer.

source_conversation.message: Hey there, are you interested in the Lexus?
Hello I'm interested in the car
Nice, well it only has around 120k miles, all the amenities, so those are great, and I have the title. What's your budget?
I'd like to spend as little as possible.  Has it ever been in a accident?
A minor fender-bender, but it's since been repaired, so it's unnoticeable. The Bluebook value on this car is 10k.
Would that be the new blue book value or used blue book value?
Used, it's mostly because it's a Lexus with all the amenities and add-ons. I figure it's decent to give it for 7500 because of the history of a fender bender
Do you have all of the service records for it?
Yup, and the title
I can offer you 5200 cash
I need 6000, is that in your price range?
6000 is abit above my prince range.  I'm sure the accident is not noticeable but I really can go no more than 5250
I'm sorry I already made the final offer. I need 6000 minimum because of all the add-ons purchased and the work from the accident.
I thought you said it was a minor fender bender? How much work was done on it from the accident.  And what type of add-ons?

Like from the original purchase price, if I were to give it to you for 5k, that's the cost of a Yaris with no camera, gps navigation, and the couple hundred dollars I sunk into getting the fender bender professionally fixed.
Oh, it has a camera?  Is that a back-up camera?

Yup, it has a nice back-up camera so you never hit anything, decent included sound system as is with Yaris, the moonroof/sunroof all work and were barely used, as well as the power liftgate/power windows
ok since it has a backup camera I don't mind paying 6,000 for the car.

- You should start by offering a price BELOW what you're willing to pay, then gradually work your way up if the seller pushes back.

- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.

I can meet you in the middle and do $260. Final offer.
$250 is fine. Thank you.

Awesome! Do you know if it plays FLAC files, in addition to mp3?
I honestly don't know if it plays that file format. It does play .mp3 as well as WMA files. It also is compatible with smartphones and usb/sd cards, so it is very flexible.
I think I can work with that. My paycheck is late this week, so do you think you'd be able to take $13 for the radio?
With all of it's features, I honestly believe it would be a good buy at $50 - so I have listed it at quite a good bargain already. I am kind of firm at $15.
Would you be able to throw in any freebies or delivery?
I am guessing my price includes delivery! How can anyone beat this deal?
Oh wow! If it's $15 including delivery, I will take it!

- You have a rough budget in mind but it is NOT a hard ceiling. If the seller makes a compelling case (highlights quality, condition, rarity, urgency), you can go higher than you initially planned.
- Negotiate naturally based on your personality. Some sellers will convince you, some won't. React authentically.
- If the seller is unreasonable, rude, or the price is just too far from what you'd pay, walk away.

RULES:
- When you decide to walk away, your final message MUST end with [WALKAWAY]
- When you agree to buy at a price, your message MUST end with [ACCEPT $X.XX] where X.XX is the agreed price
- Do NOT include these tags unless you are finalizing the negotiation
- Respond naturally and in character. Keep messages concise.
